title: Listening - Part 3: Shopping at a Market
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a market they enjoy visiting. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for liking the market. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a market they enjoy visiting. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for liking the market. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about a market they enjoy visiting.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I used to buy fruit and bread in a supermarket without thinking where they came from. At the Saturday market, several stalls are run by farms and bakeries within a few miles of the town. [19]I enjoy buying there because I can give my money directly to people producing food in the local area, rather than choosing goods transported a long distance by a large company.[/19] It sometimes costs slightly more, but the food is fresh and the stallholders remember regular customers.||Speaker 2 Man: I started growing herbs and small vegetables on my balcony, although I knew almost nothing about which plants would manage well in containers. The garden centre sold plenty, but staff were often too busy to talk. [20]At the market, one seller takes time to ask about the amount of sun my balcony gets and tells me exactly how to care for each plant before I buy it.[/20] Thanks to her, I now have healthy tomatoes and mint instead of pots containing only dry leaves.||Speaker 3 Woman: The monthly antiques market is enormous, and much of what is sold there doesn't interest me at all. I'm looking for old music records, particularly jazz, and sometimes go home empty-handed. [21]However, occasionally I search through a box and find an excellent record for a tiny price because the seller hasn't realised how difficult it is to obtain elsewhere.[/21] That possibility makes every visit exciting. Even if I find nothing, I enjoy the search, but discovering a bargain is the real attraction.||Speaker 4 Man: There's an evening market by the river every summer. I don't usually buy much, because most of the crafts are beyond my budget and I prefer cooking at home. [22]I keep going because of the music, the lights, the smell of different foods and the mixture of people walking around and stopping to chat on warm evenings.[/22] It feels as though the entire town is outside enjoying itself. Even when I leave with empty hands, I've had a cheerful night out.||Speaker 5 Woman: I work most weekends, which means I miss the farmers' markets my friends visit on Saturday mornings. Then I discovered a small market held on Wednesday nights, just after my office closes. [23]What suits me is that I can walk there after work and buy fresh food for the next few days without needing to rearrange my schedule or rush out early at a weekend.[/23] The stalls aren't as numerous as at bigger markets, but being able to attend regularly is far more useful.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I used to buy fruit and bread in a supermarket without thinking where they came from. At the Saturday market, several stalls are run by farms and bakeries within a few miles of the town. I enjoy buying there because I can give my money directly to people producing food in the local area, rather than choosing goods transported a long distance by a large company. It sometimes costs slightly more, but the food is fresh and the stallholders remember regular customers.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: I started growing herbs and small vegetables on my balcony, although I knew almost nothing about which plants would manage well in containers. The garden centre sold plenty, but staff were often too busy to talk. At the market, one seller takes time to ask about the amount of sun my balcony gets and tells me exactly how to care for each plant before I buy it. Thanks to her, I now have healthy tomatoes and mint instead of pots containing only dry leaves.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: The monthly antiques market is enormous, and much of what is sold there doesn't interest me at all. I'm looking for old music records, particularly jazz, and sometimes go home empty-handed. However, occasionally I search through a box and find an excellent record for a tiny price because the seller hasn't realised how difficult it is to obtain elsewhere. That possibility makes every visit exciting. Even if I find nothing, I enjoy the search, but discovering a bargain is the real attraction.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: There's an evening market by the river every summer. I don't usually buy much, because most of the crafts are beyond my budget and I prefer cooking at home. I keep going because of the music, the lights, the smell of different foods and the mixture of people walking around and stopping to chat on warm evenings. It feels as though the entire town is outside enjoying itself. Even when I leave with empty hands, I've had a cheerful night out.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I work most weekends, which means I miss the farmers' markets my friends visit on Saturday mornings. Then I discovered a small market held on Wednesday nights, just after my office closes. What suits me is that I can walk there after work and buy fresh food for the next few days without needing to rearrange my schedule or rush out early at a weekend. The stalls aren't as numerous as at bigger markets, but being able to attend regularly is far more useful.

options.text: the chance to support local producers
the opportunity to improve language skills

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker likes spending money directly with food producers from the surrounding area.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker values guidance from a seller about plants suitable for her balcony.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The speaker enjoys the chance of finding valuable music records at very low prices.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker visits principally to enjoy the market's sociable and lively evening mood.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The market's weekday evening schedule makes it practical for the speaker to visit after work.
